persons.arguments_against: Single-initial warning plus 3 Gascoigne biographies in the corpus invite the namesake/relative hypothesis: a different J. Gascoigne in Bermuda. It fails: the stint office is Governor and Commander-in-Chief of Bermuda, a unique single-occupant post, and the biography records 'gov. and c.-in-c., Bermuda, 1959-64', enclosing the stint's 1960-1964 listing. The staff list prints 'Major-Gen. Sir Julian Gascoigne' — full forename, knighthood, and the bio's exact rank — and the honour record is year-accurate: C.B. and D.S.O. before 1962, K.C.M.G. added in the 1962 staff list, matching the bio's 'K.C.M.G. (1962)'. A second Major-Gen. Sir Julian Gascoigne with the same honours governing Bermuda in the same years is not a live possibility.
persons.would_overturn: External evidence that a different person held the Bermuda governorship 1960-1964, or that the bio's final clause was conflated from another entry.

notes: position_sim FAIL is the standard abbreviation artifact. Independent agreeing dimensions: unique office with tenure containment (1959-64 vs 1960-64), full forename + rank, three shared honours with a year-accurate K.C.M.G. appearance in 1962.
